instruction: Write a package main HTTP handler that wires an http.ServeMux (standard library only, no third-party router) for GET /kv/{key} and PUT /kv/{key} backed by an in-memory store. Use the Go 1.22+ method+wildcard route patterns and r.PathValue. PUT returns 201 Created, GET returns the value (200) or 404 if absent. Expose newMux(s *Store) *http.ServeMux.

Write a package main table/flow test using net/http/httptest for a key/value HTTP service built with newMux(NewStore()): PUT a key returns 201, GET it back returns 200 with the stored body, GET a missing key returns 404. Standard library only.

Write a package main stdlib-only JSON HTTP endpoint: POST /echo decodes a JSON body with encoding/json, validates it, and responds with JSON plus Content-Type application/json; 405 for non-POST, 400 for invalid/empty input, 200 on success. Expose newMux() *http.ServeMux.

Write a package main test using net/http/httptest and encoding/json for a JSON POST /echo endpoint built with newMux(): a valid message returns 200 with the echoed value and correct length; an empty message returns 400.
